criteria_verification.evidence: Updated DispatchFn in src/forge/cli/_serve_daemon.py from Callable[[bytes], Awaitable[None]] to Callable[[_MsgLike], Awaitable[None]] and re-exported via __all__. _process_message now passes msg (not msg.data) to dispatch_payload. Existing test seams in tests/forge/test_cli_serve_daemon.py were migrated to receive `msg: Any` rather than bytes; new TestDispatchFnSignature class asserts the type alias references _MsgLike and the default dispatcher's signature takes one parameter named `msg`.
criteria_verification.notes: Updated DispatchFn in src/forge/cli/_serve_daemon.py from Callable[[bytes], Awaitable[None]] to Callable[[_MsgLike], Awaitable[None]] and re-exported via __all__. _process_message now passes msg (not msg.data) to dispatch_payload. Existing test seams in tests/forge/test_cli_serve_daemon.py were migrated to receive `msg: Any` rather than bytes; new TestDispatchFnSignature class asserts the type alias references _MsgLike and the default dispatcher's signature takes one parameter named `msg`.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `_process_message` does not call `msg.ack()` on the success path;
criteria_verification.evidence: Removed the post-dispatch `await msg.ack()` from _process_message's success path in src/forge/cli/_serve_daemon.py. The function now only calls await dispatch_payload(msg) and returns; ack happens only in the except Exception branch. Verified by TestProcessMessageAckSemantics::test_success_path_does_not_ack which monkey-patches dispatch_payload to a no-ack coroutine and asserts msg.acked is False.
criteria_verification.notes: Removed the post-dispatch `await msg.ack()` from _process_message's success path in src/forge/cli/_serve_daemon.py. The function now only calls await dispatch_payload(msg) and returns; ack happens only in the except Exception branch. Verified by TestProcessMessageAckSemantics::test_success_path_does_not_ack which monkey-patches dispatch_payload to a no-ack coroutine and asserts msg.acked is False.

criteria_verification.evidence: _process_message's except Exception branch now calls await msg.ack() (best-effort, swallowing ack failures to debug log) and only then emits the WARNING log line. The ordering is asserted by TestProcessMessageAckSemantics::test_failure_path_acks_before_logging which inspects msg.acked plus caplog after a raising dispatcher. Existing TestDispatchFailureIsolated::test_dispatch_exception_acks_and_continues continues to pass after seam migration.

The ordering is asserted by TestProcessMessageAckSemantics::test_failure_path_acks_before_logging which inspects msg.acked plus caplog after a raising dispatcher. Existing TestDispatchFailureIsolated::test_dispatch_exception_acks_and_continues continues to pass after seam migration.

criteria_verification.evidence: Added MAX_ACK_PENDING = 1 module constant and set it on the ConsumerConfig in _attach_consumer. Rewrote the docstring to explain the strict-serial-processing rationale (ADR-ARCH-014) and removed the previous 'does not gate on max_ack_pending=1' sentence. Also added the operational rollout note in the module docstring referencing `nats consumer rm` before deploy. Verified by TestMaxAckPendingIsOne (two test methods).
criteria_verification.notes: Added MAX_ACK_PENDING = 1 module constant and set it on the ConsumerConfig in _attach_consumer. Rewrote the docstring to explain the strict-serial-processing rationale (ADR-ARCH-014) and removed the previous 'does not gate on max_ack_pending=1' sentence. Also added the operational rollout note in the module docstring referencing `nats consumer rm` before deploy. Verified by TestMaxAckPendingIsOne (two test methods).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `_default_dispatch` logs + acks itself (preserves the test seam)
criteria_verification.evidence: Replaced _default_dispatch in src/forge/cli/_serve_daemon.py: signature is now (msg: _MsgLike), it parses the envelope (acks + drops on parse failure), logs the structured info line, then calls await msg.ack(). Docstring rewritten to describe the new role as the test-seam default rebound by TASK-FW10-007. Updated TestDefaultDispatchHandlesMalformed to use _FakeMsg and assert .acked is True after the call.
criteria_verification.notes: Replaced _default_dispatch in src/forge/cli/_serve_daemon.py: signature is now (msg: _MsgLike), it parses the envelope (acks + drops on parse failure), logs the structured info line, then calls await msg.ack(). Docstring rewritten to describe the new role as the test-seam default rebound by TASK-FW10-007. Updated TestDefaultDispatchHandlesMalformed to use _FakeMsg and assert .acked is True after the call.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `_run_serve` opens **one** NATS client and shares it with all
criteria_verification.evidence: Refactored _run_serve in src/forge/cli/serve.py to call _serve_daemon.nats_connect(config.nats_url) exactly once, then pass the resulting client into run_daemon via the new client= keyword. run_daemon was extended to accept client: Any | None = None and consume the injected client on its first attach iteration; only reconnects after broker drop go through the seam. Verified by TestRunServeBootOrder::test_single_nats_connect_call_on_startup (asserts exactly one connect call and that the same client is passed to run_daemon) and TestRunDaemonAcceptsInjectedClient::test_first_attach_uses_injected_client_no_seam_call (asserts the seam is never invoked when a client is injected).
criteria_verification.notes: Refactored _run_serve in src/forge/cli/serve.py to call _serve_daemon.nats_connect(config.nats_url) exactly once, then pass the resulting client into run_daemon via the new client= keyword. run_daemon was extended to accept client: Any | None = None and consume the injected client on its first attach iteration; only reconnects after broker drop go through the seam. Verified by TestRunServeBootOrder::test_single_nats_connect_call_on_startup (asserts exactly one connect call and that the same client is passed to run_daemon) and TestRunDaemonAcceptsInjectedClient::test_first_attach_uses_injected_client_no_seam_call (asserts the seam is never invoked when a client is injected).

criteria_verification.evidence: Added two module-level rebindable seams in src/forge/cli/serve.py: recovery_reconcile_on_boot and consumer_reconcile_on_boot, both Callable[[client], Awaitable[None]]. _run_serve awaits both sequentially (recovery first, then consumer) BEFORE creating the daemon_task. Production wiring for the deps graph is owned by later FW10 tasks per the brief, so the defaults are intentional logged no-ops. Ordering verified by TestRunServeBootOrder::test_both_reconcile_on_boot_run_before_daemon which records the call sequence and asserts connect < both reconciles < daemon_started.
criteria_verification.notes: Added two module-level rebindable seams in src/forge/cli/serve.py: recovery_reconcile_on_boot and consumer_reconcile_on_boot, both Callable[[client], Awaitable[None]]. _run_serve awaits both sequentially (recovery first, then consumer) BEFORE creating the daemon_task. Production wiring for the deps graph is owned by later FW10 tasks per the brief, so the defaults are intentional logged no-ops. Ordering verified by TestRunServeBootOrder::test_both_reconcile_on_boot_run_before_daemon which records the call sequence and asserts connect < both reconciles < daemon_started.

criteria_verification.evidence: Added chain_ready: bool = False to SubscriptionState in src/forge/cli/_serve_state.py with a guarded async set_chain_ready setter, lock-free is_chain_ready reader, and a composite is_healthy() helper. _run_serve flips it via await state.set_chain_ready(True) after both reconcile_on_boot calls return. healthz handler in _serve_healthz.py now checks chain_ready first and returns 503 with reason='chain_not_ready' until it flips. Verified by TestRunServeBootOrder::test_chain_ready_flips_true_after_reconciles (records chain_ready around recovery vs daemon callbacks), TestSubscriptionStateChainReady (three tests), and TestHealthzChainReadyGate::test_row1_chain_not_ready_is_unhealthy_even_if_live.
criteria_verification.notes: Added chain_ready: bool = False to SubscriptionState in src/forge/cli/_serve_state.py with a guarded async set_chain_ready setter, lock-free is_chain_ready reader, and a composite is_healthy() helper. _run_serve flips it via await state.set_chain_ready(True) after both reconcile_on_boot calls return. healthz handler in _serve_healthz.py now checks chain_ready first and returns 503 with reason='chain_not_ready' until it flips. Verified by TestRunServeBootOrder::test_chain_ready_flips_true_after_reconciles (records chain_ready around recovery vs daemon callbacks), TestSubscriptionStateChainReady (three tests), and TestHealthzChainReadyGate::test_row1_chain_not_ready_is_unhealthy_even_if_live.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Healthz reports unhealthy if the NATS subscription drops, even
criteria_verification.evidence: _healthz_handler returns 503 with reason='subscription_not_live' when chain_ready is True but is_live() is False. Row 3 of the Group E scenario outline is asserted by TestHealthzChainReadyGate::test_row3_chain_ready_subscription_dropped_is_unhealthy; rows 1 and 2 are also covered (chain_not_ready and full healthy paths). Existing integration test TestStateTransitionsAreReflected::test_503_then_200_after_set_live_true was updated to set chain_ready up-front and exercises the live-flip transition through the gate.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All existing F009-003 daemon tests pass after the seam migration.
criteria_verification.evidence: Migrated existing test_cli_serve_daemon.py monkey-patch sites (TestUnackedOnCrash, TestDispatchFailureIsolated) to the new (_MsgLike) -> None dispatch signature. Updated _default_dispatch test to construct _FakeMsg objects and assert ack semantics. Updated test_cli_serve_skeleton.py and test_cli_serve_logging.py fakes to accept the new client= keyword and to monkey-patch _serve_daemon.nats_connect so _run_serve does not try to reach a real broker. Updated test_serve_healthz.py existing tests to set chain_ready alongside live where the test exercised the live-only gate. Final pytest run: 4057 passed, 5 skipped, 0 failed across the whole tests/ tree.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 345 chars provided.
context_used:
## Job-Specific Context

Budget: 1679/5200 tokens

### 🎨 Relevant Patterns
- INSTRUCTS_USER_TO_START
- IS_USED_IN
- ASSIGNS_TASK_TO
- HAS_ERROR_HANDLER_FOR

### ⚠️ Warnings
- HAS_ROOT_CAUSE
- DEPENDS_ON
- ERROR_CAUSE_OF

### 🎭 Role Constraints
- DELEGATE_TO_TASK_WORK
- HAS_ROLE_MUST_DO
- DELEGATES_TO

### 🛠️ Implementation Modes
- WHEN_TO_USE
